title: Supply Chain, Browser Binaries, Packages, Containers, and Rollout · Narrowing Workshop
summary: A disability-led public-interest technology team incrementally builds source-bound least-authority crawler release from original deterministic HTTP, robots, sitemap, HTML, browser, and report fixtures while retaining prior TypeScript, Node, web, Git, accessibility, security, testing, and recovery requirements. Planned artifact: Build a complete first version of source-bound least-authority crawler release while every earlier competency remains active. Learners progress through prediction, expli…
objectives: Review direct purpose, transitive graph, lifecycle scripts, integrity, provenance, licenses, advisories, reachability, and upgrade ownership.
Bind Playwright package, browser revision, download source, digest, platform libraries, sandbox policy, and patch process.
Inspect packed files, exports, declarations, source maps, licenses, fixtures, secrets, generated files, and emitted smoke before publish.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Authorized site auditing and TypeScript Commit to the mission model · Workshop Dependency Provenance
steps.instruction:
workshop case — A workshop TypeScript team handles case 5196 during a narrow-screen keyboard and screen-reader review. Build deterministic TypeScript evidence to prove dependency and browser provenance, package contents, least-authority containers, and immutable rollout; every external value remains unknown until runtime admission produces an owned value; then inject one authenticated, private-data, output-injection, path, log, plugin, or correction fault and contain it. Browser TypeScript uses pure bounded models and original URL, robots, sitemap, HTTP, HTML, frontier, accessibility, browser, and report fixtures only. Real DNS, Fetch, streams, Cheerio, Playwright, axe-core, files, processes, packages, containers, assistive-technology review, load, legal review, restore, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates.

What must be established before implementation?

Evidence target: Authorized site auditing and TypeScript Commit to the mission model · Workshop Dependency Provenance.
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for review direct purpose, transitive graph, lifecycle scripts, integrity, provenance, licenses, advisories, reachability, and upgrade ownership. For Authorized site auditing and TypeScript Commit to the mission model · Workshop Dependency Provenance, keep Dependency Provenance connected to executable TypeScript authorization, URL, runtime admission, Fetch, Promise, browser, accessibility, report, package, and recovery decision evidence.

lines.label: assumption
lines.text: A workshop TypeScript team handles case 6815 during a multilingual archive markup migration. lines.label: risk
lines.text: Rebuilding from the same commit produces the same approved release.

lines.label: evidence
lines.text: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Promote one immutable artifact through canary scopes, compare correctness and resource evidence, pause automatically, and rehearse rollback. Artifact under review: source-bound least-authority crawler release.

options.text: The record names the assumption, applies Progressive Rollout, and reports a changed-case check tied to the stakeholder decision.

The Progressive Rollout record says source-bound least-authority crawler release looks right but supplies no governing condition, observation, changed case, or counterexample. That claim fails the audit the initial evidence packet evidence boundary for Progressive Rollout.
